events.description: Commissioner of Indian Affairs sent circular letter to superintendents regarding patents-in-fee
Board of Indian Commissioners wrote to Commissioner enclosing replies regarding patent-in-fee Indians
Board of Indian Commissioners Bulletin No. 147 published regarding releasing Indians from Government supervision
Superintendent Asbury compiled statistics on 301 Indians regarding disposition of property of 140 patent-in-fee Indians
Approximately 600 patents-in-fee issued beginning prior to 1915 to mixed-bloods and educated Indians
Competency Commission issued patents to mixed-blood element, returned soldiers, and successful industrial workers
Mack Johnson given patent to 40 acres of bottom land
Unnamed Indian (Kickapoo or similar) died in Mexico after selling patented land and squandering money, leaving destitute family
Board of Indian Commissioners wrote to Commissioner Charles H. Burke enclosing remaining field letters on patents-in-fee
Board of Indian Commissioners Bulletin No. 152 published with remaining field letters
84 of 140 patent-in-fee Indians (60%) sold all of their land

events.name: mortgage_record
events.description: 10 of 54 land-holding patent-in-fee Indians reported as having mortgages
Mrs. Amy Scott lost mortgaged cattle and ranch at sheriff's sale
Mrs. Olive R. Venne's automobile, truck and engine sold at sheriff's sale after mortgage

financial_transactions.description: Joseph Cooper sold land after receiving fee patent several years prior
Henry Reid sold patented land and purchased automobile

financial_transactions.amount: proceeds used for high priced automobile
financial_transactions.description: Ralph Saco sold patented land, later traded automobile for second hand Ford to satisfy garage repair charges

financial_transactions.amount: sufficient to buy Ford and little over
financial_transactions.description: Ben Gardner sold land near oil well
Mrs. Amy Scott secured mortgage on cattle ranch to buy cattle, later mortgaged cattle
Mrs. Olive R. Venne mortgaged home to buy automobile, motor truck, and engine
Mortgaged items sold at loss to satisfy debts
40 acres of bottom land patented to Mack Johnson valued at $3000-$4000
Sold patented land, spent money, died in Mexico
Two Indians who died mortgaged places after receiving patents

legislative_actions.outcome: Provided for original allotments at Standing Rock
Provided for allotment of wives' lands
Removed restrictions on surplus allotments for Five Civilized Tribes
Automatic removal of restrictions on all land of restricted Indians
